## [00:00 - 03:00]

Okay, so this is actually really, really important, but probably one of the things that most Facebook freelancers either have no idea how to do or you don't really learn about this stuff. When you start working with clients or other agencies or other partners on the same account, this is going to become even more important. I'm talking about naming conventions. So naming your campaigns and your ad sets. And you know what? Even your ads, even though I'll admit I'm really bad at naming ads at the ad level, so that anyone on your team can quickly identify what's what, your clients can identify which campaigns are from you, which ones were from them, and you can organize things in a way that makes sense. So as you already know the way that you should be structuring your campaigns is that at the campaign level, you'll have the campaign objectives, so your conversions based on that funnel step. So at the top, you might have a video so that campaign objective would be video views, and then you might have lots of different ad sets within that campaign targeting a look alike, targeting an interest audience, targeting all sorts of different audiences, right? And testing out at the ad set level. And then under the ad set level, you're going to have all the different creative for each of those ad sets. So what's important though is naming each of those so that you understand which ones which. Now what we use and what we recommend in the academy is what we call Toph, Moth and Boff. So top of funnel, middle of funnel and bottom of funnel as you'll see here. Top of funnel, middle of funnel, bottom of funnel. So what you're looking at here is for example, this is a top of funnel campaign. And this is going out to a cold audience and we're using gamification here. Top of funnel using a webinar, top of funnel using another piece of creative, middle of funnel, we've got PPEs going and then bottom of funnel, we've got a retargeting stack happening. So this isn't to say this is the perfect funnel or this is the funnel that that's besides the point here. It's just understanding or letting you recognize that you should be using naming conventions to quickly organize your campaigns. So you'll see here, we've got Toph, Moth and Boff. And then we actually have what the campaign is. And we have the either the lead magnet or the asset that's being used, if it's important or if it's differentiated for that client. That may not always be the case depending on what you're doing. So for example,

e-commerce that might not be the case. But for e-commerce, you might decide top of funnel, it might be a video for a specific product, you know, that you're pushing. And then you might have another one for another specific product that you're pushing as well. Now, when we go down to the just one last thing here at the campaign level, what we would even do if it was a different account. So this is obviously my account here. But if this was a client account, we would always add this to be the beginning. So eight L and obviously this is an abbreviation for eight loop. And this means that we can tell right away which campaigns we created and which ones the client had created or which campaigns someone else created. So everything with eight L, we can instantly recognize as our own. Highly recommend you create an abbreviation for your own agency so that you can also do the same. So what you'll see here at the ad set level is similar, similar situation. Now we've got a mobile desktop and we've got a different placement happening here and two different targeting audiences. So the way that we structure this is we have the country that we list out. So it might be a U U S the audience. So we might say custom audience or look alike. In this case, this is a power editor plus interest to audience. And then we've got the placement listed here as well. So I can quickly see right away when I'm looking at my stats. Okay, well, desktop's doing a lot better. Mobile is doing a lot better. It's really easy for me to identify right away which placements, which audiences, which countries are working really well, instead of having to go into each one of them or bring them up at the reporting level, right? Just the naming conventions allows me to look at my stats and quickly assess, okay, this country is going well, this one, not so much. This placement works. This one, not so much. And then at the ad level, well, I don't really do naming conventions so much, but if you wanted to, you could do naming conventions at the ad level. And you would just list out either what that post ID is or what that ad name is. So if it's a specific creative that you're, you're running through the, the different campaigns or ad sets.
